Free Self-Assessment — No Strings Attached

You Deserve Relationships ThatLift You Up.

You deserve relationships that lift you up — not ones that leave you exhausted, drained, and questioning your worth.

The relationships in your life — at work, at home, in love, in friendship — don't just surround you. They shape who you become. This book gives you the tools and the roadmap — whether you're navigating a draining marriage, a suffocating workplace, or a family dynamic that leaves you depleted. Take the free self-assessment to see exactly where you stand. 10 honest signs. 5 clear steps forward.

The Self-Assessment

10 Signs to Check

Drawn from the book's framework. Read each one. Check what resonates — even a little.

The Criticism Cascade — They constantly point out what you do wrong, what you've failed at, or why you're not enough. Praise is rare, conditional, or comes with a sting.
The Gaslighting Test — When you raise a concern, you end up doubting yourself by the end. "You're overreacting." "You're imagining things." You've started trusting their version over your own.
The Eggshell Walk — You've become expert at reading their mood before you speak. You adjust your tone, words, even body language to avoid setting them off.
The Guilt Loop — When you express a need or draw a boundary, you somehow end up apologizing. Your needs become the problem.
The Blame Transfer — They never quite own a mistake…
The Empathy Void — Your emotions are inconvenient…
The Silent Weapon — Silence used strategically…

+ 7 more signs, your score, and 5 steps forward

About the Book

Simple Yet Profound. Genuinely Challenging.

Designed to be visually clear, quickly read, and deeply practical — all to help readers identify toxic patterns, reclaim their power, and build relationships rooted in mutual respect.

Whether you're currently in a difficult dynamic or healing from one, every page has something to help you move forward.

The quality of your life is shaped by the quality of every relationship in it — not just the romantic ones. This book covers it all: love, work, family, friendship.

Spot Hidden Toxicity

Learn to recognize emotional traps — from guilt trips to gaslighting — before they can do more damage

8 Clear Steps

A structured roadmap from awareness to thriving

Real Scripts

Word-for-word language for hard conversations

Reflection Tools

Quizzes, journal prompts, and action checklists

7Introduction
Introduction

Surround yourself with only people who are going to lift you higher.

Oprah Winfrey

What if you surrounded yourself only with those who lift you higher? Could your relationships transform from sources of stress to wellsprings of joy? This book isn’t just about cutting ties; it’s about cultivating a life where every connection empowers you.

Thriving is about flourishing in a space of self-love and nurturing connections that respect and enrich both parties involved, as well as escaping the clutches of negativity.

My own odyssey from the depths of toxic relationships to the tranquility of self-awareness and reciprocal love is a narrative shared by many. This book was born from my struggles and victories…

continued inside the book…

Inside the Book

174 Pages. 8 Chapters.

174 Pages 8 Chapters Reflection Prompts Action Checklists
  • BE AWARE · p.11Recognize the 20 subtle signs of toxic relationships — including those that look like love. Reflection questions to evaluate your relationships clearly.
  • BE INSIGHTFUL · p.31Uncover attachment styles and childhood patterns pulling you back. Tools for self-discovery and lasting change.
  • BE EMPOWERED · p.53Build real self-worth through self-care and boundary setting. Guided meditation for self-empowerment included.
  • BE PROACTIVE · p.73Word-for-word scripts for hard conversations. Role-playing exercises for real-life scenarios.
  • BE COURAGEOUS · p.95Stay and transform or leave safely — decision-making flowcharts and preparation checklists included.
  • BE HEALED · p.119Move through grief, restore self-trust, and unlock healing through guided journaling.
  • BE OPEN · p.133Build relationships rooted in trust. Vision board exercise and guide to your authentic self.
  • BE COMMITTED · p.145Craft your personal resilience plan and turn every challenge into lasting strength.

Why This Book Exists

"I wrote the book I wish I'd had when I was in the middle of it — when I couldn't see clearly, when I kept making excuses, when I thought maybe I was the problem."

For a long time, the normalizing of toxic patterns felt like just... life. In relationships, in the workplace, in family dynamics. Like so many people, there wasn't a name for what was happening. Just the sense that something felt wrong and the inability to figure out how to make it stop.

Through years of research, honest self-reflection, and personal work that actually changed everything, the path forward emerged — and eventually, a marriage rooted in mutual respect, genuine partnership, and love that doesn't cost you yourself.

This book is for every reader who has thought "maybe I'm the problem" one too many times. It's practical, honest, and built from lived experience — not just theory. What actually worked, structured into 8 clear steps anyone can follow.

What Changes

Three Outcomes. Real Results.

The assessment is the beginning. Here's what the full 8-step framework delivers.

01
Spot Hidden Patterns

Toxic patterns don't always look toxic. They show up as self-doubt, constant apologizing, and the quiet erosion of identity. Self-reflection quizzes help uncover the childhood wounds and unconscious habits pulling readers back — and finally see clearly enough to break the cycle.

02
Set Firm Boundaries

Most advice tells you what to do — not how to actually say it. Get word-for-word scripts for the conversations you've been dreading: the partner who guilt-trips, the family member who dismisses, the colleague who crosses lines. Say no without apologizing.

03
Rebuild and Thrive

Getting out is only the beginning. Rebuilding means coming back fully to yourself. Through journal prompts, grief rituals, and self-compassion practices, restore the self-trust toxic relationships quietly dismantle — and step into a life where your worth is non-negotiable.

About the Author

E.W. Rye

Author · Strategist · Speaker

"I normalized things I had no business normalizing. I thought if I tried harder, gave more, shrank a little further — it would work. It never did. And I didn't have a book like this to tell me why."

Rye knows this territory from the inside. For years, she experienced the kind of dynamics she now writes about — the emotional exhaustion, the self-doubt, the slow erosion of knowing who you are.

Through research, personal work, and the kind of honest reckoning that changes you, Rye found her way to clarity — and to a marriage rooted in mutual respect, genuine partnership, and love that doesn't cost her herself.

As an author, strategist, and speaker, Rye draws on both research and lived experience to help readers do the same. Her approach blends research-based insights with practical, actionable tools — because theory without application doesn't change lives.

Your Path Forward

From Exhausted to Empowered

Most people spend years in pain trying to figure this out alone. This book gives you the map.

1

Name What's Happening

Start with the free assessment. 10 patterns that are easy to miss because they've been normalized. If even 3 sound familiar, this book is for you.

→
2

Understand the Root

Chapter by chapter, uncover why you stayed, why it felt like love, and what patterns from your past made you vulnerable to this dynamic.

→
3

Use the Tools

Scripts for hard conversations. Decision-making flowcharts. Journal prompts. Checklists. Every chapter ends with an action, not just an insight.

→
4

Rebuild — for Good

Restore self-trust. Set boundaries that hold. Build relationships rooted in real respect. This time, you'll recognize red flags before they become patterns.

174 pages. 8 chapters. Reflection prompts in every chapter. Written for anyone ready to stop settling.
